==Chinese==
{{zh-forms|s=丧|alt=䘮,𠸶,𠷔,𡂤,𡂧,𡴧,𦦦,𡚏,𦦭,𦱺}}

===Glyph origin===
{{Han etym}}
Originally {{Han compound|桑|㗊|c1=p|c2=s|t1=mulberry tree|t2=many mouths|ls=psc|nocap=y}}. The number of mouths frequently varied. Sometimes {{zh-l|*走|run}} was included as a semantic component, and later {{och-l|亡|die}} was added as a phonetic (and also possibly semantic) component.

In the {{w|seal script}} became {{Han compound|哭|亡|c1=s|c2=p|t1=cry|t2=die|ls=psc|ls2=ic|nocap=y}}, from which the modern form derives.

Compare {{zh-l|*咢}} and {{zh-l|*噩}}, which may be derived from or originally the same character as 喪.

===Etymology===
From {{inh|zh|sit-pro|*ma|t=not}}. Cognate with {{och-l|亡|to disappear}}. The level-toned pronunciation has a nominal prefix ''*s-'', ''lit.'' "circumstances associated with disappearance (death)", whereas the departing-toned pronunciation has a formally identical causative prefix ''*s-'' and an exoactive suffix ''*-s'', ''lit.'' "to cause to disappear".
